name: Rugelach
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream margarine with cream cheese in a large bowl using an electric mixer.
2. Gradually add flour and mix well.
3. Divide dough into four parts and chill wrapped in waxed paper for 3-4 hours.
4. To shape: Roll out one portion on a floured surface, cut into circles with a round cookie cutter.
5. Place half a teaspoon of raspberry filling mixture in the center of each circle. Bring the sides towards the center or fold in half to form a crescent.
6. Roll next portion of dough into a rectangle and spread chocolate filling mixture onto it.
7. Roll third portion of dough into another rectangle, covering the chocolate layer.
8. Cut into small squares using the last portion of dough, placing a teaspoonful of apricot filling mixture in the center.
9. Top with half a dried apricot and bring the sides towards the center. If desired, roll the remaining dough into a large circle and cut into triangles.
10. Spread apricot, preserve, and nut mixture on triangles.
11. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 12-13 minutes while still hot.
12. Roll in confectioner's sugar immediately after baking and let cool.
13. Store in airtight containers until ready to serve. Re-roll in confectioner's sugar just before serving.
14. Makes approximately 40 cookies. Keep in airtight containers at room temperature.
